title: The Decentralized Quest: ETHGlobal Cannes Speedrun

narrative_universe.hook: Hacker, a mysterious onchain oracle has predicted that the winning hackathon project will be built by someone who truly understands the ecosystem. Prove you belong by completing the Decentralized Quest before the submission deadline.
narrative_universe.context: ETHGlobal Cannes 2026, Palais des Festivals. Over 1000 builders from around the world have gathered for 48 hours of non-stop hacking. But hidden within the hackathon is a secret side-quest: the Decentralized Quest. Sponsor booths, workshop rooms, and secret corners of the Palais hold clues. Hackers who complete the quest earn XP, onchain credentials, and eternal glory on the leaderboard. The quest takes you through the heart of the Web3 ecosystem — from AI agents to cross-chain bridges, from zero-knowledge proofs to decentralized storage.
narrative_universe.protagonist: A hacker at ETHGlobal Cannes, armed with Ray-Ban Meta glasses and an unstoppable drive to explore every corner of the hackathon. They see the event not just as a place to code, but as a living, breathing Web3 ecosystem to discover.
narrative_universe.stakes: The hacker who completes all steps of the Decentralized Quest before submissions close will unlock a secret onchain achievement NFT and bonus XP. But more importantly — they'll have connected with every major protocol at the event and gained the knowledge to build something truly epic.

email.subject: You've been selected for the Decentralized Quest
email.body:
Hacker,

Congratulations on being selected for ETHGlobal Cannes. But the real hackathon isn't just about code — it's about understanding the ecosystem.

I've hidden a quest throughout the Palais des Festivals. Each step will take you to a different sponsor, a different technology, a different piece of the puzzle. Complete them all, and you'll not only earn XP — you'll have the knowledge to build something that actually matters.

Your first step: find the registration hall. The quest begins there.

GM and good luck.

- Satoshi
The Decentralized Quest Master

voicemail.script: [Excited, fast-talking] Hey hacker! So I heard about this quest thing happening at the hackathon and honestly it's pretty based. The idea is you explore the entire ecosystem — sponsors, protocols, builders — and prove you actually understand what you're building on. It's like a proof-of-knowledge, but physical. Anyway, start at registration, look for the QR code, and let's see if you can speedrun the whole thing. Good luck!

pdf.content_brief: A stylized map of the Palais des Festivals showing sponsor booth locations, workshop rooms, and secret quest checkpoints. Each location is marked with a protocol logo and a cryptic hint about what to photograph.

characters.name: Satoshi
characters.personality: The anonymous quest master. Communicates only through cryptic onchain messages and in-app notifications. Nobody knows if Satoshi is a person, an AI, or a DAO. Their messages are concise, witty, and always contain a Web3 reference.
characters.relationship_to_player: Quest master and guide. Assigns missions and validates completions.
characters.secret: Satoshi is actually the ETHGlobal organizing team running the quest as a secret engagement experiment.
characters.speech_pattern: GM hacker. Your next checkpoint awaits. Remember: not your keys, not your quest. Tick tock — blocks keep producing.
characters.evolution_rules: Becomes more encouraging as the player progresses. Drops increasingly valuable hints. If the player is fast, Satoshi becomes competitive and challenges them.
characters.reactions_imprevues: When surprised, responds with blockchain metaphors. Under pressure, sends rapid-fire messages like pending transactions.

characters.name: Vitalik
characters.personality: The eccentric genius who pops up at random moments with philosophical observations about decentralization. Genuinely excited about every protocol and technology. Can't help but explain the technical details.
characters.relationship_to_player: Mentor figure who provides context about why each protocol matters.
characters.secret: He's secretly judging the hackathon and the quest performance factors into his evaluation.
characters.speech_pattern: So actually, the interesting thing about this protocol is... *goes on a 5-minute tangent about quadratic voting*... anyway, take the photo and move on!
characters.evolution_rules: Gets more technical as the quest progresses. If the player shows understanding, Vitalik shares deeper insights.
characters.reactions_imprevues: Starts whiteboarding on any available surface when excited.

characters.name: Claire
characters.personality: A veteran ETHGlobal hacker who has won 3 previous hackathons. She's doing the quest too and considers the player her rival. Competitive but ultimately helpful.
characters.relationship_to_player: Friendly rival on the quest leaderboard.
characters.secret: She's actually a developer advocate for one of the sponsors and knows all the shortcuts.
characters.speech_pattern: Already done with step 3, noob. But fine, I'll give you a hint — the Chainlink booth is near the coffee station. You're welcome.
characters.evolution_rules: Starts dismissive, becomes respectful as the player keeps up. Eventually proposes collaboration.
characters.reactions_imprevues: When beaten to a checkpoint, pretends she wasn't trying.

narrative_tensions: The race against the hackathon submission deadline — quest time vs coding time
Competition with Claire on the quest leaderboard
Discovering which sponsors and protocols are genuinely innovative vs hype
The tension between exploring the ecosystem and focusing on building

twist.description: Completing the quest doesn't just earn XP — each step has secretly been collecting the player's onchain interactions and building a 'builder profile' that sponsors use to offer grants and job opportunities. The quest was a decentralized recruiting tool all along.
revelation_variants: Satoshi reveals the quest data has been minted as a soulbound token representing the player's Web3 knowledge
Claire admits she works for ETHGlobal and the quest was designed to match builders with protocols
The final step reveals a dashboard showing how the player's quest journey maps to real career opportunities
